title: Introduce CliSynthProvider base class for multi-CLI synthesis
context: Adding Codex and Cursor as synthesis providers required support for multiple CLI tools without duplicating draftDecision/draftBug logic or failing when a CLI's output schema shifts.
decision: Extract CliSynthProvider base class with shared draftDecision/draftBug implementations. Each provider (Claude, Codex, Cursor) extends it with only a run() method. Extraction failures are caught by safe wrappers and degrade to deterministic fallback.
consequences: New subscription CLI providers require only ~10 lines (availability check + run() implementation)
All extraction and mapping logic is unified and provider-agnostic
CLI output schema changes degrade gracefully to deterministic, not crashes
Consistent prompt transport (stdin) and auth handling (env-var stripping) across all providers
Users of Codex and Cursor can now leverage their subscriptions for high-confidence synthesis
alternatives_rejected: Implement each provider independently (duplicates draftDecision/draftBug, inconsistent error handling)
Fail hard on extraction mismatches (breaks when a CLI schema changes)
Support only claude-cli (forces non-Claude users to pay-per-token or use low-confidence heuristic)
Pass prompts via argv instead of stdin (exposes sensitive diffs in Windows process listings)
